subject: Mozi Minute: Close Rate Pricing Guide
body:
Words I like:

Dear younger self→The less a customer pays, the more problems they cause.

Mozi Minute: Close Rate Pricing Guide

I see businesses everywhere obsess over closing more deals when they should be obsessing over charging more money.

Your close rate isn't just a sales metric - it's a pricing diagnostic. And most businesses are leaving cash on the table by ignoring it.

This is my close rate pricing guide cheat sheet (for services):

80%+ close rate → You're underpriced by 3-4x (triple or quadruple your prices)

60-80% close rate → You're underpriced by 2-3x (double or triple your prices)

50-60% close rate → You're underpriced by 1.5-2x (increase prices 50-100%)

40-50% close rate → You're underpriced by 1.25 to 1.5x (increase prices 25-50%)

30-40% close rate → You're probably appropriately priced (maintain)

Below 30% → Focus on selling better before touching price

The math is simple:

If you're closing 8 out of 10 prospects, you're not charging enough. Period. Don't believe me? Test it. Double your price with the next 10 prospects and see what happens.

I've seen this play out with too many companies to count. That being said, if you are in a commoditized market - your prices will be more sensitive. So the key isn't to make the tiny change - the key is to figure out how to not be a commodity - then make a massive change.

Reminder:

The correct price is one in which you hear "no" more than "yes", but you make more money.

As promised, value in under a minute.

Want to find your optimal price point without sacrificing revenue?

We just opened spots for our next Scaling Workshop in Vegas.

We turn away 65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Raise prices, raise profits, Alex
